name: use-key
title: useKey
description: Bind keyboard shortcuts to handlers with scoped, document-level, or element-targeted listening. Supports single key, array of keys, or key map overloads.

content.text: register is useEffectEvent-based (stable reference), excluded from deps

docs.description: Bind keyboard shortcuts to handlers with scoped, document-level, or element-targeted listening. Supports single key, array of keys, or key map overloads.

parameters.name: hotkey
parameters.description: A single key combo, an array of key combos, or a map of key combos to handlers.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: Callback invoked when the hotkey fires. Not used with the key-map overload.

parameters.name: options.allowInInput
parameters.description: Allow the hotkey to fire when an input, textarea, or contenteditable element is focused.

parameters.name: options.targetRef
parameters.description: Scope listening to a specific DOM element instead of the document.

parameters.name: options.requireFocusWithin
parameters.description: Only fire the handler when focus is within the target element.

parameters.name: options.preventDefault
parameters.description: Call preventDefault() on the keyboard event when the hotkey matches.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.

notes.title: Three overloads
notes.content: useKey supports three call signatures: single key + handler, array of keys + handler, and a Record<string, KeyHandler> map (no separate handler argument).

notes.title: Requires KeyboardProvider
notes.content: useKey is a provider-dependent hook. Wrap your component tree with <KeyboardProvider> before using it.

notes.title: Scope-aware
notes.content: Handlers respect the scope stack managed by useScope. Deeper scopes take priority over shallower ones.

use-key-basic.raw:
"use client" import { useState } from "react" import { KeyboardProvider, useKey } from "@diffgazer/keys" import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" import { Kbd } from "@/components/ui/kbd" function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0) useKey("ArrowUp", () => setCount((c) => c + 1)) useKey("ArrowDown", () => setCount((c) => c - 1)) useKey("Escape", () => setCount(0)) return ( Counter

{count}

0 ? "success" : count < 0 ? "error" : "neutral"} dot> {count > 0 ? "Positive" : count < 0 ? "Negative" : "Zero"}

↑ increment {" "}↓ decrement {" "}Esc reset

) } export default function UseKeyBasic() { return ( ) }

use-key-map.raw:
"use client" import { useState } from "react" import { KeyboardProvider, useKey } from "@diffgazer/keys" import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" import { Kbd, KbdGroup } from "@/components/ui/kbd" function TextFormatter() { const [style, setStyle] = useState("normal") useKey({ "ctrl+b": () => setStyle("bold"), "ctrl+i": () => setStyle("italic"), "ctrl+u": () => setStyle("underline"), Escape: () => setStyle("normal"), }) const badgeVariant = style === "normal" ? "neutral" as const : "info" as const return ( Text Formatter

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

{style}

CtrlB bold {" "}CtrlI italic {" "}CtrlU underline {" "}Esc reset

) } export default function UseKeyMap() { return ( ) }

content.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

parameters.name: hotkey
parameters.description: A single key combo, an array of key combos, or a map of key combos to handlers.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: Callback invoked when the hotkey fires. Not used with the key-map overload.

parameters.name: options.allowInInput
parameters.description: Allow the hotkey to fire when an input, textarea, or contenteditable element is focused.

parameters.name: options.targetRef
parameters.description: Scope listening to a specific DOM element instead of the document.

parameters.name: options.requireFocusWithin
parameters.description: Only fire the handler when focus is within the target element.

parameters.name: options.preventDefault
parameters.description: Call preventDefault() on the keyboard event when the hotkey matches.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.
